name: Dispatch & Fan-out
description: Consolidated routers, the run-with-flags wrapper, profile gating, and bootstrap adapters that read stdin and fan each Claude Code lifecycle event out to the individual hook checks.

name: Blocking Gates
description: PreToolUse/Stop hooks that can exit 2 and deny an action — blocking --no-verify bypasses, fact-forcing destructive ops, protecting linter/formatter configs, blocking foreground dev-server commands, and failing low-quality commits.

name: Observation & Telemetry
description: Non-blocking PostToolUse/Stop hooks that aggregate session metrics, track cost and context usage, render the statusline, audit-log commands and PRs/builds, and extract reusable patterns — pure observation that never alters tool results.

name: Quality & Formatting
description: PostToolUse/Stop hooks that lint, format, type-check, and flag console.log or generic-template drift on edited files, warning the agent (with the Stop format/typecheck step able to gate).

name: Session Lifecycle & Context
description: SessionStart/SessionEnd/Stop/PreCompact hooks that inject startup context, persist and clean up session state, manage and suggest compaction, and wire the Cursor session environment.

name: Security
description: Security-focused hooks: the Insaits security monitor with its bridge wrapper, governance-event capture for secrets and policy violations, and the MCP health check.

name: Nudges & Shared Output
description: Non-blocking reminders (git push, tmux), transparent dev-server tmux rewriting, ad-hoc doc-file warnings, desktop notifications, and the shared additionalContext output helper used across the PreToolUse nudges.
